subject: [PATCH v5 20/21] swapfile: replace the swap map with bitmaps

2026-03-20.patch_summary: This patch replaces the swap map in the Linux kernel's swapfile code with two bitmaps, one for tracking allocated swap entries and another for bad swap entries. This change is made possible by moving the swap count state to the virtual swap layer, which reduces the number of states that need to be tracked from 4 to 3. The use of bitmaps instead of a single array saves 6 bits per swap entry, reducing memory usage. The patch updates the swap_info_struct structure in include/linux/swap.h and modifies several functions in mm/swapfile.c to work with the new bitmap-based representation.
